full_prompt: Design the wing root fitting of an XV-15-baseline hybrid-electric tiltrotor benchmark, interfacing the cantilever wing box to the fuselage carry-through structure. Aircraft gross weight 6577 kg (14500 lb), rotor diameter 7.62 m, two wing-tip proprotors each generating 35,600 N of hover thrust in helicopter mode, rotor blade count 3. Wing root fitting must transfer: (a) rotor thrust reacted vertically in helicopter mode and forward in airplane mode during conversion, (b) wing bending from aerodynamic lift in cruise, (c) nacelle-tilt actuator reaction moment during the 12-second conversion from helicopter to airplane mode. Fitting material: Ti-6Al-4V forging (Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Fitting envelope: 500 mm fore-aft x 300 mm chord-wise x 250 mm vertical, with a main pin interface (OD 80 mm bore) to the nacelle spindle, four M20 bolt holes to the wing box spar at a 150 x 120 mm pattern, and two M24 bolts to the fuselage frame. Load cases: (LC1) helicopter hover OGE: vertical lift 35,600 N per rotor down on the fitting through the nacelle spindle pin; (LC2) conversion 50% airplane: cyclic couple 8000 N-m plus 25,000 N thrust at 45 deg; (LC3) airplane cruise: forward thrust 7000 N + wing bending moment 120 kN-m out-of-plane at root; (LC4) OEI hover at 1220 m: single-rotor thrust up to 42,000 N with lateral sway load 8000 N; (LC5) hard-landing: 4 g vertical at DGW (258 kN across both wing fittings, 129 kN per side); (LC6) fatigue: 10^7 cycles of +/- 30% hover load plus conversion cycle spectrum. Pass/fail per MIL-HDBK-516C + FAR 29 rotorcraft: (R1) max von Mises across LC1-LC5 <= 633 MPa (ultimate 950 / 1.5); (R2) max stress at limit <= 748 MPa (yield 860 / 1.15); (R3) pin bore ovalization under LC1 <= 0.15 mm; (R4) fatigue at fillet radius <= 120 MPa 10^7 cycle endurance; (R5) fitting mass <= 32 kg; (R6) first in-plane mode above 25 Hz. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, modal, fatigue, mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the wing root fitting of a hybrid-electric tiltrotor (XV-15 baseline aerodynamics), transferring rotor thrust, wing bending, and nacelle-tilt actuator reaction between the cantilever wing box and the fuselage carry-through during the 12 s helicopter-to-airplane conversion. Aircraft DGW 6577 kg, wing-tip proprotors 35,600 N hover thrust each, three blades. Material class: Ti-6Al-4V forging. Qualify per MIL-HDBK-516C + FAR Part 29 rotorcraft, with ultimate FoS 1.5 and yield FoS 1.15.

Envelope: fitting on the order of 500 mm fore-aft x 300 mm chord-wise x 250 mm vertical, with an 80 mm nacelle-spindle pin bore, a four-bolt wing-box pattern, and two bolts to the fuselage frame.

Load cases.
- LC1 helicopter hover OGE: 35,600 N vertical down through the nacelle spindle pin.
- LC2 conversion at 50% airplane mode: 8000 N-m couple + 25,000 N thrust at 45 deg.
- LC3 airplane cruise: 7000 N forward thrust + 120 kN-m out-of-plane wing root bending.
- LC4 OEI hover at 1220 m: 42,000 N up + 8000 N lateral sway.
- LC5 hard landing: 4 g vertical at DGW, 129 kN per side fitting.
- LC6 fatigue: 1e7 cycles of +/- 30% hover load plus the conversion spectrum.

prompt.title: Hybrid-electric tiltrotor wing root fitting
prompt.brief: Ti-6Al-4V root fitting transferring rotor + wing + conversion loads to fuselage.
